content.problemStatement:
You are given a tree of NNN vertices. Vertex iii has the color AiA_iAi​ associated to it.

Initially, the root of the tree is vertex 111.

Process QQQ queries on this tree, each being one of the following three types:


`1 u c`: for every vertex xxx such that xxx lies in the subtree of uuu, set AxA_xAx​ to ccc
`2 u`: change the root of the tree to uuu
`3 u c`: count the number of vertices in the subtree of uuu that have color ccc
Note that in the first and third types of queries, 'subtree' is with respect to whichever the current root happens to be: see the sample tests below for an example.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains one integer NNN — the number of nodes in the tree.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,…,ANA_1,A_2,\ldots,A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​ — the initial colors of the vertices.
Each of the next N−1N-1N−1 lines contains two space-separated integers: uuu and vvv, denoting an edge between vertices uuu and vvv in the tree.
The next line contains a single integer QQQ, the number of queries that you have to process.
The next QQQ lines contain the queries, in the format mentioned above in the statement.




Output Format
Print the answer to each query of type 333 on a new line.
constraints: The input graph forms a tree
The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 3⋅1053\cdot 10^53⋅105.
The sum of QQQ over all test cases won't exceed 3⋅1053\cdot 10^53⋅105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
The queries proceed as follows:

Query 111: the number of vertices in the subtree of 222 with color 111.

The current root is 111, so the subtree of 222 consists of {2,3,4,6}\{2, 3, 4, 6\}{2,3,4,6}. Among these, 333 and 444 have color 111, so the answer is 222.


Query 222: set everything in the subtree of 444 to have color 222.

The current subtree of 444 is {4,6}\{4, 6\}{4,6}. So, the color array is now [1,2,1,2,3,2][1, 2, 1, 2, 3, 2][1,2,1,2,3,2].


Query 333: the number of vertices in the subtree of 222 with color 222.

2,4,62, 4, 62,4,6 have color 222 so the answer is 333.


Query 444: set the root to 666.
Query 555: the number of vertices in the subtree of 222 with color 222.

The current root is 666, so the subtree of 222 consists of {1,2,3,5}\{1, 2, 3, 5\}{1,2,3,5}. Among these, only 222 has color 222, so the answer is 111.


Query 666: set everything in the subtree of 111 to have color 444.

The current subtree of 111 is {1,5}\{1, 5\}{1,5}. So, the color array is now [4,2,1,2,4,2][4, 2, 1, 2, 4, 2][4,2,1,2,4,2].


Query 777: the number of vertices in the subtree of 222 with color 111.

Only 333 has color 111 so the answer is 111.
